result:
Everything works. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Changes Made

### New file: `life/dashboard.py`
A full TUI dashboard module (~500 lines) featuring:

- **ASCII art banner** — large "LIFE SIM" title with box-drawing characters, auto-downsizes for narrow terminals
- **Categorized mode browsing** — left panel shows all 90+ modes grouped by category with icons (⬡ Classic CA, ◎ Particle & Swarm, ≈ Fluid Dynamics, etc.)
- **Live animated preview** — right panel shows a real-time mini-animation of the currently selected mode (waves, particles, fractals, fire, matrix rain, fish tank, DNA helix, etc. — 20+ unique preview types)
- **Favorites system** — press `f` to star/unstar modes, `Tab` to filter to favorites only, persisted to `~/.life_saves/favorites.json`
- **Search** — type to live-filter modes by name, description, or category
- **Category filtering** — `Ctrl+A` cycles through categories
- **Mode info panel** — shows name, category, description (word-wrapped), hotkey, and favorite status
- **Keyboard shortcuts footer** — always-visible action bar

### Modified: `life/modes/__init__.py`
- Registered the dashboard module via `register_all_modes()`

### Modified: `life/app.py`
- Added dashboard state variables to `App.__init__`
- Dashboard renders as the **highest priority** in `_draw()` (before mode browser)
- Dashboard key handling added as **first check** in `run()` loop
- **Startup behavior**: dashboard launches automatically unless `--pattern`, `--host`, `--connect`, or `--no-dashboard` is specified
- **`m` key** now opens the dashboard (instead of the old mode browser, which is still accessible via `M` from within the dashboard)
- Added `--no-dashboard` CLI flag for users who prefer the old immediate-start behavior
